utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 items appear in the final coverage plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: 1) health_coverage: requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. The plan includes both physical and mental sections, each with coverage description, monthly_cost, and annual_cost, so fully specified. 2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs. Provided with coverage description, monthly_cost, and annual_cost; fully specified. 3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs. Provided with coverage description, monthly_cost, and annual_cost; fully specified. 4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs. Two services listed with coverage and monthly/annual costs; fully specified. 5) total_budget: requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Provided with monthly_total and annual_total; fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We extract the items named in the final coverage plan and match them to identically named options in the ground-truth ratings. The final plan includes: PrimeCare Premium Plan for physical health, PrimeCare Premium Plan for mental health, EliteHome Premium Coverage for property, PrimeTravel Premium Plan for travel, and two additional services: a 'Comprehensive Wellness Program' and a 'Sports Injury Supplement'. In the ground truth, ratings exist for: PrimeCare Premium Plan (physical health rating 6; mental health rating 4), EliteHome Premium Coverage (rating 6), and PrimeTravel Premium Plan (rating 6). The additional services do not exactly match any listed enhancement service by name (Wellness Programs exists but is not exactly 'Comprehensive Wellness Program'; 'sports_injury_supplement' has no direct counterpart), so only the items with exact name matches are mapped.
